chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B.A. No.2875 of 2025 (Syed Sohail Sadiq vs. The State) Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz-ul-Hassan Shah For hearing of bail application Date of hearing & order 03.12.2025 O R D E R ----- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant is seeking post arrest bail in a case bearing Crime No.152/2025, u/s 9(I) 3(b) CNS Act, registered at Police Station NKIA, Karachi by means of this application. 2. Applicant was arrested on 15.03.2025 by a police team of Police Station NKIA, Karachi headed by PI Shakeel Ahmed Shaikh, from Double PMT Street, Khamisa Goth, Katchi Abadi, Sector 5/F, New Karachi, and from him allegedly 610 grams of charas was recovered. Hence, he was booked in the present case. 3. Learned counsel for the applicant has argued that applicant is first offender; investigation is complete as the challan has been submitted, and more so the alleged offence does not fall within the prohibitory clause u/s 497 Cr. PC; and that no video recording of incident was made. 4. Learned DPG has opposed bail to accused but the grounds taken in defence are worth consideration in that the investigation is complete, the applicant is the first offender, he is no more required for further investigation, and the alleged offence does not fall within the prohibitory clause u/s 497 Cr.PC. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/ (Rupees fifty thousand) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 5. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms.

Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/ (Rupees fifty thousand) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 5. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Rafiq/PA
